TO BEAT JUDGE HINKLE,

Judge Richardson Will Receive Nom Partisan


Support for Superior Bench.

pages.raw_text:

tional.


In all labor disputes brought be-
fore Judge Richardson. in which
an injunction was sought, the is
suance of the injunction never took
place until the laboring men were
given hearing. At the present
day the plan is first to issue the
injunction and have the hearing
afterward


At republican headquarters, the
fight of Judge Hinkle has been
taken up and an effort will be
made between now and election
day to make his candidacy (Which
is presumed to be nonpartisan), a
party issue and urge his support by
all republicans.


Judge Hinkle is recognized as
one of the mainstays of the party
organization in private capacity.


The contest for the election of
Judge Richardson will be waged
with vigor from this time forward
An effort will be made by circulars
and otherwise to acquaint the pub
lie with his candidacy and record
on. the bench, and his friends say
that If the movement grows in the
next few days as it has the first
three days of this week, the Judge
will be elected.
